understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get a pushable object onto a goal tile marked with '+' on the semantic grid. The game had multiple levels, and winning all levels would complete the KA59 puzzle game. In this particular level, the goal tile(s) were located at specific position(s) marked '+', and I needed to maneuver the selected piece to reach that location.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game uses a grid-based movement system where: (1) CLICK on a position teleports/moves the selected piece to that location if it's reachable, (2) MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the selected piece in that cardinal direction by a fixed distance (appears to be 3 units based on movement patterns), (3) Pieces can be blocked by obstacles or walls that prevent movement, (4) The selected piece can be moved between different positions to navigate around obstacles, (5) Each level has a step budget (in this case started at ~20+ steps and counted down to 0), (6) When steps reach 0, the run ends regardless of whether the goal was achieved. The game requires efficient path-planning to reach the goal within the step limit.
